meta.title: How Interest-Rate Swaps Unlock the Next Phase of Solana DeFi

transcript:
Hello everyone. My name is Thomas. I'm one of the co-founders of Exponent. And if you're here today at Solana Breakpoint, there's a high chance you're as bullish on Solana as we are. You've seen the network going from near death to being one of the most successful blockchain networks in the world. But if you look at Solana DeFi over the past year, we've struggled to attract significant new, net new capital onto the chain, and Solana DeFi activity has been overall pretty flat. So today I want to take the few minutes I have to walk you through what we think is missing for Solana DeFi to go to the next stage and onboard new participants like institutions and sophisticated participants, and also talk about briefly Exponent and how Exponent fits in there, and the products that we have that we think can unlock more capital onto Solana.

We've seen a lot of strong signals over the past months about a new wave of participants looking to enter Solana and deploy capital. So this wave of participants is more sophisticated, they have different needs. So I assume a lot of you have seen all the DATs like Forward and Solmate, but we've seen also leading asset managers trying to perform strategies on Solana, utilize the protocols available, and also products like Kamino Earn Vaults or Drift strategy vaults trying to enable those asset managers to share their expertise to onchain participants and allow them to access the same yields as they're making. But today the Solana ecosystem is still mostly built for individuals and retail participants, and a lot of them, after talking to those institutional players, a lot of them are missing a lot of tools and are not able to fully deploy capital across Solana DeFi.

We think there's two big barriers to them driving more growth to Solana DeFi. One of them being the high SOL inflation rate, which I won't cover much here because a lot of people from the Solana community are working on that and trying to change it. And then the second is the yield landscape today on Solana is mostly driven by variable yield options. And at Exponent we've been focusing on that to try to enable market participants to access fixed rates and, most importantly, hedge variable risk on their DeFi positions.

So quickly, to give some context on Exponent, Exponent today is Solana's leading yield exchange. We, by every metric, from volume to TVL, amount of users... we're thoroughly audited, the codebase is open source and source available... open source and verifiable. And we've seen a lot of those asset managers using Exponent and incorporating fixed rates into their portfolio. Exponent is built to enable participants to trade interest rates. And so asset managers like RockawayX, MEV Capital, DATs like DFD vs SOL, have started using interest rate swap instruments powered by Exponent for their portfolio constructions. And over the past month, that led us to work on a new iteration of the protocol to better support this next wave of participants and make sure that we can see massive inflows of institutional capital coming onto Solana.

In the coming quarter, we will be unveiling Exponent v2, which, as I've said, is really built for this next phase of sophisticated and institutional participants. And Exponent v2 is built around a few major upgrades. It's a full overhaul of the Exponent smart contracts and interface. We're going to have new liquidity primitives that reflect what you can find in traditional finance for interest rate swaps, and source more professional liquidity for yield markets. We're going to have a new strategy vault layer which will enable some of those asset managers to simplify strategies around Exponent to anyone. And then we're having a complete new UX for both pro users and normal users.

I don't have much time left sadly, but just to quickly talk about a few of those updates. With all the new liquidity primitives that we're going to launch, we're going to be able to better support the yield landscape on Solana, and we're going to have way more maturities and markets than you see today on Exponent. And that means more complexities for users, and so to alleviate that, we're going to allow a lot of professional asset managers like RockawayX and other leading curators in the space to build and sell strategies on top of Exponent and combine interest rate swaps products... instruments from Exponent with blue-chip protocols like Kamino or Exponent. With v2 and the strategy layer, we're also going to be able to offer new experiences around interest rate swaps like fixed-rate borrowing across Solana. And we're going to be able to allow users, like Kamino users, to hedge their variable yield positions. So we're going to start first with Kamino and then expand that to the whole credit landscape on Solana.

We think 2026 is going to be pretty massive for Solana DeFi in terms of growth. We expect a lot of new products around fixed-rate credit. We expect to see more of those institutional players trying to enter the space, and we believe that interest rate swaps and managed yield products are going to be at the core of this growth. If you want to learn more and stay up to date about Exponent v2, you can follow us on X at x.com/exponentfinance. We're going to start sharing more updates next month. And otherwise, we're here at Solana Breakpoint if you want to talk to us. If you're an asset manager interested into learning more about what we've been working on, and if you're also a company or project launching a yield product and interested into leveraging Exponent to attract more users, come talk to us, we're here the whole day. Thank you everyone.

# UPDATE -- Figure brings $YLDS to Solana, unlocking real RWA utility for DeFi
## $YLDS will deliver fiat on/off ramp capabilities and a yielding stablecoin to the Solana ecosystem and sets the stage for Solana-native Figure RWA applications
December 10, 2025 18:16 ET| Source:[Figure Technology Solutions, Inc.](https://www.globenewswire.com/en/search/organization/FT%20Intermediate%CE%B4%20Inc%C2%A7)FollowFigure Technology Solutions, Inc.
Share
* * * * * * * * NEW YORK, Dec. 10, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) --[Figure Technology Solutions, Inc.](https://www.globenewswire.com/Tracker?data=SO5xFwh7zHFksPIXn7Pdorr_5b5OQuSqtCkWN5JlR6dcPuNhmYdzaZKo1OGQZO9px-rnY1nNR9Eu16pI7IgI4u0HYd3HQKnjXr2qXKs2hJXdvurllMpI_xPaPqt8p_T9)(Nasdaq: FIGR) today announced that its subsidiary, Figure Certificate Company (FCC), intends to begin minting $YLDS, a registered public debt security, natively on Solana. $YLDS is a security-version of stablecoin, designed to maintain a fixed dollar price and provide a continuous yield backed by U.S. Treasuries and Treasury repo agreements.[Exponent Finance](https://www.globenewswire.com/Tracker?data=u5lnKYK8LdDZ1BsWO35PRW-u_p1f3reUUUWEm_S-kvq15rqTX1XklDgUPbT6KumIq7FzcS_UVV-uC4kCzwffPmcadYERCl40aSvcJU_5Xuo=), a decentralized finance yield exchange platform on Solana, intends to be the first user of $YLDS.
The move marks the beginning of a broader, long-term ambition between Figure and Provenance Blockchain Foundation to build on Solana. As part of its efforts to promote real-world asset applications in DeFi, Figure intends to work with Provenance Blockchain to allow Solana users to access yield from a variety of Figure assets, including loans. Over the coming weeks, Figure expects to expand this collaboration with additional Solana-native integrations and initiatives that further demonstrate its leadership in bringing compliant, decentralized finance to scale.
“We are thrilled to partner with Exponent Finance as a first mover in bringing $YLDS to the Solana ecosystem,” said Mike Cagney, Figure’s co-founder and executive chairman. “We see this as a way to future-proof fiat rails onto Solana while bringing an SEC-registered yielding stablecoin to the ecosystem.”
Builders on Solana will be able to access $YLDS for payment, cross-border remit, yield and settlement applications, including directly settling SOL trades with $YLDS. In the spirit of the partnership, Figure has begun accepting SOL as collateral for crypto-backed loans with the intent to expand this to locked SOL and adopt Figure’s liquidation protection option for SOL-backed loans.
“Real-world assets (RWAs) on Solana are quickly approaching $1B, as everything from traditional assets to real estate and more are tokenized on onchain," said Lily Liu, President, Solana Foundation. "Figure’s integration builds on that momentum and demonstrates how regulated, yield-generating assets can enhance utility across the Solana ecosystem.”
Figure believes $YLDS will deliver three key advantages to the Solana ecosystem:
* **Real-world utility:**Unlike theoretical RWA concepts, $YLDS offers immediate, practical applications. The yield-bearing stablecoin, backed primarily by U.S. Treasuries, is already powering Figure's live Democratized Prime product on the Provenance Blockchain and will soon integrate with Exponent Finance's yield exchange protocol on Solana, demonstrating tangible DeFi utility from

tags.impact_reason: While interest rate swaps are a solid addition to Solana DeFi, enabling more sophisticated strategies and attracting institutional capital, the impact is still emerging and not yet ecosystem-shifting.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a 'Financial Engine' as the semantic anchor because it physically embodies the complex process of an interest-rate swap. The machine transforms a chaotic, variable input (DeFi yields) into a stable, predictable output (fixed-yield), which is the core value proposition of Exponent v2 for attracting institutional capital. I included the diegetic holographic text '4.5%' in the homepage version to instantly communicate the concept of a 'fixed-yield instrument', the primary product and the solution mentioned in the story.
visual_idea.description: A sleek, institutional 'Financial Engine' made of polished chrome and crystalline glass stands in a dark, minimalist server room. From the left, a chaotic, flickering stream of multi-colored digital light representing volatile yields flows into the machine's intake. The machine's intricate inner workings glow as it processes the energy. From an output nozzle on the right, a single, solid, coherent beam of calm blue light emerges, perfectly stable. Near this output beam, the number '4.5%' hovers as a crisp, white hologram, representing the newly created fixed-yield. The background has a shallow depth of field, with other server racks softly blurred.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A sleek, institutional 'Financial Engine' crafted from gleaming polished chrome and pristine crystalline glass stands majestically in a dark, minimalist server room. From the left, a chaotic, flickering stream of multi-colored digital light, visually representing volatile financial yields, flows dynamically into the machine's prominent intake. The machine's intricate inner workings glow with an intense, internal light as it processes this energy, casting vibrant, complex reflections across its polished chrome surfaces and through its crystalline glass components, illuminating its mechanical depth. From a sophisticated output nozzle positioned on the right, a single, solid, coherent beam of calm, stable blue light emerges, perfectly focused and unwavering, symbolizing a newly created fixed-yield. Adjacent to this serene output beam, a crisp, ethereal white holographic projection of the number '4.5%' hovers precisely in the air, serving as a clear representation of the stable financial return. The background reveals other server racks, softly blurred with an extremely shallow depth of field, maintaining a minimalist aesthetic. The composition is strictly vertical (3:4 aspect ratio), emphasizing the immense height and epic scale of the Financial Engine, which stands monumentally with significant negative space above, conveying its grandeur. The scene is bathed in dramatic, focused lighting, creating striking contrasts between deep shadows and brilliant, vivid specular highlights on the polished chrome, which also catches warm amber reflections. The crystalline glass exhibits complex refractions and an internal glow, showcasing hyperrealistic and detailed materials. Ultra-photorealistic, 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, photoreal detail, high dynamic range, cinematic contrast.

The machine transforms a chaotic, variable input (DeFi yields) into a stable, predictable output (fixed-yield), which is the core value proposition of Exponent v2 for attracting institutional capital. I included the diegetic holographic text '4.5%' in the homepage version to instantly communicate the concept of a 'fixed-yield instrument', the primary product and the solution mentioned in the story.
visual_idea.description: A macro shot focusing on the core of the Financial Engine. We see the chaotic, flickering digital light entering a complex array of interlocking crystalline lenses and polished chrome conduits. The focus is on the moment of transformation, where the erratic energy is being converged and stabilized, just before it becomes the single, coherent beam of light. No text is visible in this version.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A macro shot focusing on the core of a Financial Engine. The scene captures the exact moment of transformation, where chaotic, flickering digital light enters a complex array of interlocking crystalline lenses and polished chrome conduits. The erratic energy is being meticulously converged and stabilized, just before it coalesces into a single, coherent beam of light. Emphasize the exquisite reflections on the polished chrome surfaces and the intricate refractions within the crystalline glass, showcasing the intense, focused light beams. The composition is a perfectly symmetrical, centrally focused, tight macro frame that fills the entire 1:1 square aspect ratio with rich texture and intricate details. No text or numbers are visible. 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, ultra-photorealistic, high dynamic range, physically based rendering, cinematic lighting, shallow depth of field, studio lighting.

editorial.headline: Exponent Unveils v2 to Bring Institutional Interest-Rate Swaps On-Chain
editorial.standfirst: The protocol overhaul addresses the lack of fixed-yield instruments, identified as the primary barrier preventing institutional capital from entering Solana DeFi. By introducing professional hedging tools and strategy vaults, Exponent aims to capture the wave of asset managers seeking predictable returns.
key_takeaways: Launch of **Exponent v2** in Q1 2026 features a new strategy vault layer for asset managers.
Partners **RockawayX** and **MEV Capital** are actively utilizing interest rate swaps for portfolio hedging.
Integration with **Kamino** will enable the first fixed-rate borrowing and variable yield hedging on Solana.
editorial.article_body_markdown:
Solana’s DeFi sector has struggled to attract net new capital over the past year, primarily because the ecosystem remains optimized for retail speculation rather than institutional allocation. **Exponent** co-founder Thomas Lefort argues that the dominance of variable yield options prevents sophisticated actors from deploying significant liquidity. To solve this, the protocol is launching **Exponent v2**, a comprehensive overhaul designed to bring TradFi-standard interest rate swaps (IRS) and fixed-rate borrowing to the chain.

The v2 upgrade introduces a "Strategy Vault Layer," a primitive that allows professional asset managers to package and sell complex yield strategies to the broader market. Firms like **RockawayX** and **MEV Capital** are already utilizing Exponent’s infrastructure to construct hedged portfolios, signaling a shift toward more mature market behavior. By enabling users to hedge variable yield positions—starting with **Kamino**—Exponent is effectively building the risk management layer required for credit markets to scale.

This infrastructure is launching against a backdrop of increasing institutional interest. With **Figure** recently announcing the migration of its $YLDS stablecoin to Solana and citing **Exponent** as a key integration partner, the demand for predictable, yield-bearing instruments is concrete. By solving for interest rate risk, Exponent is removing the final structural hurdle for institutions ready to treat Solana as a viable financial layer in 2026.
